description:
Advances: specled.verification, specled.tagged_tests

Deliverable:
Agent-legibility fix for the failure mode that produced s65.3: a single timed-out merged run rendered as '59 verification_command_failed findings', which pattern-matches for an LLM to 'the codebase is broken'. Two changes, same emission site:

1. Preserve the timeout fact instead of erasing it. lib/specled_ex/verifier.ex run_command (~line 1897) currently coerces :timeout to exit_code 1, making a hung/slow command indistinguishable from failing tests. Carry a timed_out marker on the result map and emit a DISTINCT finding code verification_command_timeout (instead of verification_command_failed) when it fires. Message must carry the remedy inline, e.g.: "command exceeded <N>ms (verification.command_timeout_ms in .spec/config.yml, default 120000); tests were not observed to fail. Re-run with --command-timeout-ms <2N> to confirm this is a budget problem." Non-timeout failures keep verification_command_failed unchanged.
2. Say the fate is shared. Findings produced from the merged tagged_tests result (one command distributed to every participating subject, verifier.ex ~1847) must state event multiplicity in each per-subject message, e.g.: "aggregated tagged_tests run (1 command shared by <N> subjects) — this finding reflects the shared run, not a subject-specific failure." Applies to both the timeout and failure variants of the merged-run finding.

Spec work (this is spec-governed behavior):
- .spec/specs/verification.spec.md: REVISE specled.verify.command_timeout_enforced — statement currently says a non-zero exit code shall be recorded on timeout; revise to: the timeout shall be recorded distinctly on the result and surfaced as verification_command_timeout, while still marking the verification failed for gating purposes. Note: the same requirement's 'the spawned process shall be killed' clause is aspirational today (Port.close does not kill the child) — do NOT attempt to fix process reaping here; it is stage 1 of the streaming-attribution epic. Add requirement + scenario for the new finding code.
- .spec/specs/tagged_tests_verification.spec.md: new must requirement + scenario for shared-fate message context on merged-run findings. Do NOT touch specled.tagged_tests.merged_run_attribution semantics — the single result is still distributed to all entries; only the message content changes.
- Author a short .spec/decisions/ entry only if the requirement revision is judged architecturally significant; otherwise skip.
- Regenerate .spec/state.json via mix spec.check.

Depends on specled_-s65.6 (the remediation message references --command-timeout-ms; land the flag first).

Verification:
- Tests to pass: additions to test/specled_ex/verifier_test.exs — timeout produces verification_command_timeout (not _failed) with budget + remediation in message; non-timeout non-zero exit still produces verification_command_failed; merged-run findings carry the shared-by-N context in every participating subject's finding; N matches the participating entry count. Tag with the new/revised requirement ids.
- Behaviors to demonstrate: the scenarios authored in this ticket.
- Commands: mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test && mix format --check-formatted && mix deps.unlock --check-unused && mix spec.check

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- lib/specled_ex/verifier.ex
- test/specled_ex/verifier_test.exs
- .spec/specs/verification.spec.md
- .spec/specs/tagged_tests_verification.spec.md
- .spec/decisions/** (new file only, if warranted)
- .spec/state.json

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/mix/tasks/**
- lib/specled_ex/tagged_tests.ex
- .spec/config.yml

Out of Scope (intent):
- No process-group kill / orphan reaping (streaming-attribution epic stage 1).
- No per-subject attribution changes — one merged result, distributed to all, exactly as today.
- No new severity semantics: verification_command_timeout gates the same as verification_command_failed (fails the run); only the code and message differ.
- No formatter, no artifact, no resume logic.
- Conventions: to_timeout/1 not :timer helpers; escape interpolation in doc strings.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly (mix compile --warnings-as-errors)
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] mix spec.check green
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: codex-44e5dfd2
Log: /Users/mz/src/specled_ex-timeout-distinct-finding/.codex-runs/specled_-s65.7.log

Commit: 643bc24dd2fa1cdc195a59e0ccba1a582fcb84fe
Files touched: .spec/specs/tagged_tests_verification.spec.md, .spec/specs/verification.spec.md, .spec/state.json, lib/specled_ex/verifier.ex, test/mix/tasks/spec_check_test.exs, test/mix/tasks/spec_tasks_test.exs, test/specled_ex/mix/tasks/spec_validate_test.exs, test/specled_ex/verifier_test.exs
Tests added: SpecLedEx.VerifierTest "command verification captures non-zero exit codes"; SpecLedEx.VerifierTest "command verification times out on slow commands"; SpecLedEx.VerifierTest "tagged_tests merged command failure surfaces a finding per subject"; SpecLedEx.VerifierTest "tagged_tests merged command timeout surfaces shared context per subject"; Mix.Tasks.Spec.ValidateCommandTimeoutTest "absent flag falls back to config value"; Mix.Tasks.Spec.CheckCommandTimeoutTest "absent flag falls back to config value"; Mix.Tasks.SpecTasksTest "spec.validate honors command timeout from config"; Mix.Tasks.SpecTasksTest "spec.check honors command timeout from config"
Verification stubs flipped: none

Summary: Command results now preserve timeout state with a timed_out marker and emit verification_command_timeout with the configured budget and --command-timeout-ms remediation, while ordinary non-zero exits still emit verification_command_failed. Aggregated tagged_tests failure and timeout findings now include shared-run context for every participating subject without changing the single-result distribution semantics. Specs and generated state were updated for the new timeout finding and shared-fate message contract.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: 80aca0d3-aeb1-4290-be1c-a32b31ea51b6 (subagent agent-averify-specled-s65-7-8573187981915201)
Log: /Users/mz/.claude-remembers/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/80aca0d3-aeb1-4290-be1c-a32b31ea51b6/subagents/agent-averify-specled-s65-7-8573187981915201.jsonl

NOTE: the immediately preceding "-F" comment on this ticket is a stray posted by me
(a `bw comment -F` invocation error — bw has no -F flag and no per-comment delete). Ignore it.

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 8
- Files outside Allowed globs: 3
  - test/mix/tasks/spec_check_test.exs, test/mix/tasks/spec_tasks_test.exs,
    test/specled_ex/mix/tasks/spec_validate_test.exs
  - WHY: each held a PRE-EXISTING s65.6 timeout scenario asserting `verification_command_failed`.
    This ticket renames the timeout code to `verification_command_timeout`, which necessarily
    breaks those assertions. Diff in all 3 is ONLY: (a) expected code string _failed -> _timeout,
    (b) added message-content assertions (command exceeded Nms / --command-timeout-ms 2N),
    (c) added `specled.verify.command_timeout_distinct_finding` to the @tag spec list.
    No new test cases, no unrelated logic. Confirms the shared hypothesis.
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs (lib/mix/tasks/**, lib/specled_ex/tagged_tests.ex,
  .spec/config.yml): 0

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 2 spec-verification blocks (kind: tagged_tests, execute:true), passed: 2
  (executed via mix spec.check; both new req ids reach strength=executed, meets_minimum=true)
- Requirements coverage:
  - specled.verify.command_timeout_distinct_finding: covered by test/specled_ex/verifier_test.exs:959.
    Would fail if a timeout emitted verification_command_failed or dropped the budget/--command-timeout-ms remediation.
  - specled.verify.command_timeout_enforced (revised): covered by same test (verifier_test.exs:959) --
    asserts status=="fail" + distinct timeout finding. Would fail if timeout stopped gating or was
    coerced back to a non-zero exit -> _failed.
  - specled.tagged_tests.shared_run_finding_context: covered by verifier_test.exs:1156 (merged failure)
    and :1221 (merged timeout). Would fail if any per-subject finding lacked "shared by N subjects" /
    shared-run context or if N were wrong.
  - specled.tagged_tests.merged_run_attribution (unchanged): verifier_test.exs:1156 asserts one result
    distributed to both {alpha,beta}. Would fail if distribution changed.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none.
  - No process reaping: run_command timeout clause (Port.close -> :timeout) is byte-identical to base
    bd92a6e; only the exit_code/timed_out computation below it changed.
  - No attribution change: single `result` distributed via unchanged Enum.reduce; only added
    Map.put(:tagged_tests_shared_count, length(entries)) before distribution.
  - No new severity: timeout & failure both emit severity "error" and yield status=fail.
- Project verification: PASS (compile --warnings-as-errors clean; 827 tests 0 failures; format clean;
  deps.unlock --check-unused clean; spec.check status=pass errors=0 warnings=0). state.json regeneration
  is deterministic (clean tree after re-running spec.check).
- Manual repro of timeout vs non-timeout + shared-fate: PASS.
  - Timeout (3 subjects, 1ms budget, real mix): 3 verification_command_timeout (alpha/beta/gamma),
    0 _failed, status=fail; msg had "command exceeded 1ms", config key, "default 120000",
    "--command-timeout-ms 2", "tests were not observed to fail", and "shared by 3 subjects".
    N=3 proves count is from length(entries), not hardcoded.
  - Failure (2 subjects, exit 2 shim, 30s budget): 2 verification_command_failed (alpha/beta),
    0 timeout, status=fail; shared-fate "shared by 2 subjects" present; NO timeout language leaked.
- Audit-expanded scope: none observed.

Verdict: PASS

Notes (advisory): The 3 out-of-scope test-file touches are a legitimate NECESSARY consequence of this
ticket's finding-code rename, not scope creep. Recommendation: treat as scope-expansion-worthy (allow),
NOT a bounce -- bouncing would force either a red suite (violating the merge gate) or a trivial
mechanical follow-up ticket. The touches add no new test cases and no logic. (Decision is yours.)
Minor: the shared-fate message says "shared by N subjects" where N = length(entries) (tagged_tests
entry count); the word "subjects" only exactly matches when each subject has one tagged_tests entry
(the tested/normal case). Matches the ticket's literal example wording. Advisory only, not a gate.

AUDIT: APPROVE
Session: 80aca0d3-aeb1-4290-be1c-a32b31ea51b6 (subagent agent-aaudit-specled-s65-7-77c54d0bab3bda13)
Log: ~/.claude-remembers/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/80aca0d3-aeb1-4290-be1c-a32b31ea51b6/subagents/agent-aaudit-specled-s65-7-77c54d0bab3bda13.jsonl

Reasoning: Independently re-ran the full gate on 643bc24 off base bd92a6e: compile --warnings-as-errors clean, 827 tests / 0 failures, format clean, deps.unlock --check-unused clean, spec.check status=pass errors=0 warnings=0, and state.json is deterministic (tree clean after regen; only untracked .codex-runs/ remains). All four affected requirements reach strength=executed with meets_minimum=true in state.json — genuinely consumed by tagged tests, not merely defined.

Each must-requirement is load-bearing tested:
- specled.verify.command_timeout_distinct_finding: verifier_test.exs:959 asserts the timeout emits verification_command_timeout with "command exceeded 50ms", the config key, "default 120000", "--command-timeout-ms 100" (2N), "tests were not observed to fail", AND `refute Enum.any?(findings(report, "verification_command_failed"))`. Reverting the code to coerce timeout->exit 1 / _failed breaks this test.
- specled.verify.command_timeout_enforced (revised): the revised statement correctly DROPS the false "process shall be killed" clause (Port.close does not reap, per ticket) and requires distinct recording + gating-fail without a non-zero-test-failure reading. Applied to verifier_test.exs:959 via the file's @moduletag (line 5-28 lists command_timeout_enforced); that test asserts status=="fail" + distinct _timeout + refutes _failed, so a violation fails it. Not a bare tag.
- specled.tagged_tests.shared_run_finding_context: verifier_test.exs:1156 (merged failure) and :1220 (merged timeout) each assert subject_ids=={alpha,beta} and every per-subject message contains "aggregated tagged_tests run (1 command shared by 2 subjects)" + "this finding reflects the shared run, not a subject-specific failure". Dropping the context or the distribution fails these.
- specled.tagged_tests.merged_run_attribution (unchanged): statement byte-unchanged in the spec; verifier.ex only adds `Map.put(:tagged_tests_shared_count, length(entries))` BEFORE the unchanged single-result Enum.reduce distribution.

Scope of the 3 out-of-Allowed files (test/mix/tasks/spec_check_test.exs, test/mix/tasks/spec_tasks_test.exs, test/specled_ex/mix/tasks/spec_validate_test.exs): LEGITIMATE necessary consequence, NOT scope creep. Read all three diffs directly — each change is exactly (a) expected code string verification_command_failed -> verification_command_timeout on a PRE-EXISTING s65.6 timeout scenario, (b) added message asserts ("command exceeded Nms", "--command-timeout-ms 2N") which STRENGTHEN (not weaken) the test, (c) @tag expanded to a list adding command_timeout_distinct_finding. Zero new test cases, zero logic changes, zero assertion weakening. Leaving them unchanged would red the suite and violate the merge gate. Allow.

Intent-level scope: clean. (1) No process-group kill/reaping — run_command's timeout branch only computes timed_out?/exit_code=nil; the Port.close path is byte-identical to base; no kill added. (2) Attribution unchanged — single result still distributed to every entry. (3) No new severity — both _timeout and _failed emit severity "error" and yield status=fail. Also confirmed exit_code=nil on timeout does not break downstream consumers (all `exit_code == 0` sites and the exit_code= header are guarded behind command_timed_out? or evaluate nil==0 safely), and command_exit_code_recorded stays honest (verifier_test.exs:944 still asserts exit_code=2 -> _failed and now refutes _timeout leakage).

Advisory (not a gate, agree with verifier): message says "shared by N subjects" where N=length(entries); "subjects" is exact only when each subject has one tagged_tests entry — matches the spec requirement's "entry count" and the ticket's literal example. Automated tests only exercise N=2; N=3 was covered by the verifier's manual repro, confirming N is length(entries) not hardcoded. No fix required.

Verdict: APPROVE. The VERIFY comment's coverage claims, scope counts, and intent analysis all check out against the actual diff and a fresh gate run; no missed Gap, no rubber-stamped stub.
APPROVED. Merged into specled_-s65/realization-enabled-tiers. Closing per orchestrator sign-off.

title: Timeout-distinct finding + shared-fate context in merged tagged_tests messages
